warning: This migration is not 1:1; each apparent mapping requires a behavioral decision and an owner.

entrypoint.trace: Trace stream through graph.astream and explain why the separately defined build_agent path is latent rather than active behavior.

gaps.title: Prompt replacement versus composition
gaps.source_guarantee: Describe which LangChain dynamic prompt can replace the earlier request prompt.
gaps.target_behavior: Describe how Pydantic AI instructions compose at the actual model boundary.
gaps.workaround: Use ordered before_model_request hooks to replace the latest instructions and omit boundary-only fields in the application history serializer.
gaps.decision: State the intended final prompt policy and whether the difference is preserved or adapted.
gaps.probe: Capture both model boundaries for two dependency values and a history continuation.
gaps.residual: The serializer and replacement order need success, failure, cancellation, and continuation tests whenever history processing changes.
gaps.owner: prompt-policy owner responsible for model-visible instructions

gaps.title: Local retry versus model-call retry
gaps.source_guarantee: Describe the LangChain local retry and its exact model-call and tool-call counts.
gaps.target_behavior: Describe the Pydantic AI model-correctable retry and its extra model call.
gaps.workaround: Wrap tool execution with Hooks.on.tool_execute and retry the same handler arguments for selected transient service exceptions.
gaps.decision: Assign transient service retry and model-correctable retry to separate policies.
gaps.probe: Run a fail-once tool and count model calls, tool calls, arguments, latency, and failures.
gaps.residual: Backoff, timeout, exhaustion, observability, and side-effect idempotency remain application retry-policy responsibilities.

gaps.title: Interrupt restart versus deferred approval
gaps.source_guarantee: Describe LangGraph node restart and positional interrupt correlation after resume.
gaps.target_behavior: Describe Pydantic tool-call ID correlation and the missing principal authorization boundary.
gaps.workaround: Use deferred tool requests with a server-owned pending-action record that binds call ID, principal, arguments, dependencies, and consumption state.
gaps.decision: Keep principal, tenant, issued call, original arguments, and one-time consumption server-side.
gaps.probe: Reject unknown, foreign-principal, and consumed IDs before effects, then approve exactly once.
gaps.residual: Long waits and process recovery still require a durable workflow or transactional application store.

gaps.title: Run completion versus first streamed output
gaps.source_guarantee: Describe when the source graph considers execution and its final result complete.
gaps.target_behavior: Describe how the first matching streamed output can commit before all tool work completes.
gaps.workaround: Use run_stream_events, iter, or run with an event handler and emit terminal output only from the completed AgentRunResult.
gaps.decision: Select a streaming API whose final and side-effect semantics match the public contract.
gaps.probe: Co-emit output with a retrying tool and compare final values and model rounds for both APIs.
gaps.residual: Reconnect cursors, duplicate delivery, and backpressure remain part of the application event contract.

gaps.title: Checkpoint durability and replay
gaps.source_guarantee: Describe checkpoint state, pending work, ancestry, fork, and replay guarantees in scope.
gaps.target_behavior: Explain why message history alone does not restore durable workflow state after a crash.
gaps.workaround: Select a Pydantic AI durable integration such as DBOSAgent or TemporalAgent and keep workflow state in that runtime rather than message history.
gaps.decision: Name the durable workflow boundary and keep cutover blocked until persistence is proved.
gaps.probe: Inject crashes before and after persistence and verify resume, fork, and replay behavior.
gaps.residual: Each selected backend must prove its own restart, tool-step durability, fork, and replay semantics in production configuration.

gaps.title: Exactly-once external effects
gaps.source_guarantee: Describe replay exposure when an external side effect has an unknown outcome.
gaps.target_behavior: State that neither agent framework alone supplies exactly-once service semantics.
gaps.workaround: Put a stable business idempotency key, outcome ledger, and reconciliation protocol around the external effect.
gaps.decision: Use a stable idempotency key, outcome ledger, and reconciliation outside the agent.
gaps.probe: Crash around the external write and reconcile every unknown outcome without duplication.
gaps.residual: A provider without idempotency or queryable outcomes may remain a blocker for automatic replay.

gaps.title: Fan-out reducer cancellation and order
gaps.source_guarantee: Describe Send branches, reducer merge rules, conflict behavior, and visible result order.
gaps.target_behavior: Explain why concurrent tools or shared state can introduce cancellation and race differences.
gaps.workaround: Use Pydantic Graph branch-local outputs, an explicit typed join and reducer, source indexes for ordering, and reducer-driven sibling cancellation.
gaps.decision: Use branch-local results and an explicit typed reducer with a documented failure policy.
gaps.probe: Test slow-fast order, reducer order, sibling cancellation, partial failure, and shared-state race cases.
gaps.residual: Partial results, duplicate branches, compensation, and blocking calls that ignore cancellation need project-specific policy.

gaps.title: Stream cancellation and reconnect
gaps.source_guarantee: Describe source stream modes, cancellation cleanup, durable cursor, and reconnect behavior.
gaps.target_behavior: State which event storage, cursor, and duplicate-delivery behavior the target lacks natively.
gaps.workaround: Persist a versioned application event envelope with a run cursor and make consumers deduplicate by run and sequence.
gaps.decision: Put reconnect and duplicate handling in the application event-store contract.
gaps.probe: Disconnect a slow consumer, reconnect from a cursor, and verify duplicate and terminal delivery.
gaps.residual: The chosen event store must prove retention, atomic sequence allocation, and bounded slow-consumer behavior.

gaps.title: Middleware order and control flow
gaps.source_guarantee: Describe before, after, and wrapper order plus short-circuit and state-update behavior.
gaps.target_behavior: Explain why a flat hook list does not preserve retry, failure, interrupt, or resume nesting.
gaps.workaround: Use ordered Hooks wrapper capabilities for nested model and tool control flow, with application orchestration for graph state jumps and resume.
gaps.decision: Assign each concern to a tool, hook, capability, wrapper, or application boundary.
gaps.probe: Trace exact order across success, retry, failure, interrupt, resume, and cancellation.
gaps.residual: Hooks do not by themselves reproduce checkpointed state updates, graph jumps, or durable resume transactions.

gaps.title: Structured output transport
gaps.source_guarantee: Describe provider-native versus tool strategy selection and the source result location.
gaps.target_behavior: Describe Pydantic native, tool, and prompted output modes and their wire differences.
gaps.workaround: Select NativeOutput or ToolOutput explicitly to match source transport and adapt result.output to the public result DTO.
gaps.decision: Choose one output transport explicitly and adapt it to a framework-neutral final DTO.
gaps.probe: Test invalid output, multiple candidates, provider fallback, and output co-emitted with tools.
gaps.residual: Provider fallback and invalid-output retry behavior must be checked against the selected production model profile.

gaps.title: Return-direct transitional bridge
gaps.source_guarantee: Describe how return_direct ends the source agent after the selected tool call.
gaps.target_behavior: Explain that a LangChain bridge does not automatically preserve that stop contract.
gaps.workaround: Represent the terminal callable as a named Pydantic AI output function using ToolOutput instead of an ordinary bridged function tool.
gaps.decision: Implement terminal behavior explicitly or retain the bridge only as a named temporary gap.
gaps.probe: Compare terminal value and model-call count for native and bridge tool paths.
gaps.residual: The model contract changes to a terminal output choice, and streamed partial output must not repeat side effects.

gaps.title: Recursion thread request and tool-call limits
gaps.source_guarantee: Describe graph recursion plus per-run and persisted thread model-call limits.
gaps.target_behavior: Describe Pydantic request and tool-call units, reset scope, and failure shape.
gaps.workaround: Combine UsageLimits and RunUsage with per-thread serialization or a transactional budget reservation that is settled and reconciled after dispatch.
gaps.decision: Assign recursion, thread, request, and tool-call budgets separately without numeric substitution.
gaps.probe: Exercise every boundary, an oversized parallel batch, and repeated runs on one thread.
gaps.residual: Strict cross-process parity needs per-thread serialization or transactional reservation plus crash reconciliation and a source-compatible failure adapter.

gaps.title: Application-owned event contract
gaps.source_guarantee: Describe the source event kinds, correlation, ordering, error, and terminal semantics.
gaps.target_behavior: Explain which Pydantic events require adaptation rather than direct exposure.
gaps.workaround: Normalize both runtimes into a versioned application envelope with sequence, correlation, payload, error, and completed-run terminal fields.
gaps.decision: Define a versioned envelope with correlation, sequence, payload, error, and terminal fields.
gaps.probe: Compare normalized event sequence and terminal timing across both implementations.
gaps.residual: Framework upgrades require adapter contract tests because raw event classes and timing are not the public API.

gaps.title: Approval resume durability
gaps.source_guarantee: Describe checkpointer-owned interrupt state and the source resume transaction.
gaps.target_behavior: Explain that tool-call ID, principal, original arguments, and consumed state need storage.
gaps.workaround: Persist pending approval, Pydantic message history, dependency references, authorization, and consumption state in one durable resume workflow.
gaps.decision: Persist pending approval and resume data atomically outside the agent before accepting a result.
gaps.probe: Crash before and after approval persistence and reject foreign or already-consumed calls.
gaps.residual: The production database or workflow backend must prove atomic claims, recovery leases, and retry-safe protected effects.

gaps.title: Tool schema and authorization boundary
gaps.source_guarantee: Describe injected authenticated identity and every model-visible tool schema field.
gaps.target_behavior: Explain how schema validation differs and why authorization must remain executable code.
gaps.workaround: Place identity and policy services in typed RunContext dependencies, expose only model-choosable arguments, and authorize before service execution.
gaps.decision: Keep identity, credentials, and policy services in typed dependencies outside the schema.
gaps.probe: Diff both schemas and prove unauthorized calls fail before the protected service executes.
gaps.residual: Validation messages and JSON Schema details can vary by framework and must be diffed for every migrated tool.

gaps.title: Message history protocol conversion
gaps.source_guarantee: Describe message IDs, tool-call pairing, provider metadata, and malformed-history behavior.
gaps.target_behavior: Explain why history conversion is a protocol migration rather than a cast.
gaps.workaround: Define a versioned framework-neutral conversation DTO and a fail-closed converter for only the message shapes proved by round-trip tests.
gaps.decision: Use a framework-neutral conversation boundary and an explicit versioned conversion adapter.
gaps.probe: Test parallel tool-call history, orphan results, dangling calls, duplicates, and conversion round trips.
gaps.residual: Reasoning signatures, multimodal parts, provider metadata, and malformed tool histories remain blocked until explicitly supported.
